title: Mellow spring simmered Argentinian chestnuts Nimono
description: A tangy and aromatic Argentinian dish perfect for a weeknight dinner, featuring simmered chestnuts in a flavorful agave leaf vessel.
instructions: Combine chestnuts and water in a saucepan, cover, and bring to a boil.
Transfer chestnuts to agave leaf vessels, leaving some space between each.
In a separate pan, heat the oil over medium heat, then add the onion and garlic, cooking until softened.
Pour the onion and garlic mixture over the chestnuts, followed by the white wine and vinegar.
Cover the agave leaf vessels with a lid or foil, and simmer at 180�90C (350�96F) for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the chestnuts rest for 5 minutes before serving.
